title: USD Payload Namespace Source Contract

included: producing a direct loaded payload opinion source
reading supplied payload arc sites with source-visible payload fields
path-only internal payloads within the payload arc layer namespace
external payload arcs using already-opened supplied payload layers
asset-only payload target selection through payload layer defaultPrim
mapping payload target prims and descendants under the payload-bearing prim path
remapping path-valued field contents authored under payload specs into the payload-bearing namespace
payload-contributed prim and property opinions in composed scene coordinates
payload source provenance with semantic payload-bearing and payload target paths
payload child and property names from mapped payload specs
bounded references authored inside loaded payload layer stacks when the referenced layers are already supplied
arc-local layerRelocates authored in loaded payload layer stacks and applied to nested references introduced from those stacks
recoverable ancestral-relocate diagnostics for invalid nested relocate source paths in loaded payload layer stacks
diagnostics for missing payload layers and missing target prims
deferred: payload asset resolution and resource opening
resolver search paths, callbacks, contexts, and repository policy
explicit unloaded payload state and load rules
inherits, specializes, variants, root-layer relocates, and value clips
merging payload opinions with local, reference, inherit, variant, relocate, or specialize opinions
cross-layer payload listOp composition before supplied arc-site records
attribute value resolution, time sample interpolation, and layer offsets

model.PayloadNamespaceSource: A payload opinion source backed by supplied payload arc sites plus already-opened payload Layers. It is not the provider that merges payload opinions with stronger local/reference opinions.
model.PayloadArcSite: A payload-bearing prim path plus source-visible Payload listOp value supplied by a caller, adapter, or future arc-discovery provider.
model.PayloadArc: A source-visible Payload item from a prim's payload field, with optional asset, optional target path, offset, and scale.
model.PayloadLayerSet: Already-opened document-model Layers supplied by authored payload asset identifier.
model.PayloadMapping: The target prim path in a payload layer mapped to the payload-bearing prim path in composed scene coordinates.
model.CompositionArbitrator: The usd-composition-arbitrator provider that merges payload opinions with local/reference and other composition opinions according to LIVERPS.
model.ContributingSources: Adapter rendering of strong-to-weak ContributingOpinion records. Domain records store source identity separately from semantic spec paths.

input.payloadArcSites: target-native payload-bearing prim paths and source-visible payload listOp values
input.payloadLayers: already-opened document-model Layers keyed by authored payload asset identifier

output.payloads: direct loaded payload arcs with primPath, asset, targetPath, offset, and scale

output.exists: whether payload specs contribute a prim-like input at the path
output.childNames: composed child prim names before stage mask or active filtering
output.propertyNames: composed property names before schema fallback injection

output.exists: whether payload specs contribute an attribute or relationship at the path

input.path: absolute prim path, variant-selection prim path, or /

output.opinions: payload scene-object opinions mapped into composed scene coordinates

laws: the payload source is an arc opinion source and must not require ReferenceNamespaceSource or another prior composition provider in its domain constructor
the payload source must not merge payload opinions with local, reference, inherit, variant, relocate, or specialize opinions
the payload source consumes supplied document-model Layers for payload assets and must not open files or resolve assets
payload source records must retain payload-bearing prim paths, payload target paths, and mapped contributor paths as semantic path handles/references rather than raw strings
payload contributingSources domain data must be typed ContributingOpinion records, not composite source:path strings
LIVERPS arbitration between stronger local/reference opinions and weaker payload opinions belongs to usd-composition-arbitrator, not this payload opinion source
a payload target prim maps to the payload-bearing prim path, and descendants are mapped by replacing the target prefix with the payload-bearing prim path

path-valued field contents authored on payload prims or properties, including listOp<ObjectPath> targetPaths and connectionPaths, must be remapped by replacing the payload target prefix with the payload-bearing prim path before the fields are emitted
payload targetPaths and connectionPaths elements outside the payload target subtree are pruned in this bounded external-payload provider instead of being emitted in payload-layer coordinates

for path-only internal payloads within the same layer namespace, targetPaths and connectionPaths elements outside the payload target subtree remain in the shared composed namespace rather than being pruned
path-valued field remapping is namespace composition and does not imply full listOp composition, dictionary merging, value resolution, or layer-offset application

references authored inside a loaded payload target subtree must be expanded while the referenced layers are already supplied; these nested opinions are payload-family contributions because the payload arc introduced the loaded layer stack into the composed stage

when a nested reference is authored inside a loaded payload layer stack that has layerRelocates, the nested record must carry that layer stack's relocate namespace mapping in addition to the nested reference target mapping

arc-local relocate mappings on payload records must hide source paths, map relocated target paths back to the remote source specs, suppress stale target collisions from the referenced target layer, and preserve target-local opinions from the relocating layer as stronger than relocated source opinions

childNames and propertyNames emitted by a payload source must be derived from mapped target paths, not copied directly from payload or nested referenced layer tokens, so relocate targets, deletes, and nested relocate descendants are reflected in the exposed namespace

for loaded payloads with nested references and arc-local relocates, path-valued field remapping must use the accumulated payload/reference mapping plus the arc-local relocate mapping; paths inside deleted relocate sources are pruned
asset-only payloads use the payload layer defaultPrim; a missing defaultPrim must be diagnosed
a missing supplied payload layer must be diagnosed with the authored asset identifier
a missing payload target prim must be diagnosed with the authored asset and target path
nested payloads authored inside payload layers remain inert field data in this first provider
payload layer offsets are reported but are not applied to time samples, splines, clips, timecode-valued fields, or attribute values
for non-special field keys in this first contract, field maps expose the payload-authored field value and do not combine dictionaries, listOps, time samples, or values
adapter JSON may seed payload arc sites from a root layer for conformance tests, but domain APIs exchange payload arc records, Layer, path, token, and field value objects

adapter_protocol.input: A JSON file containing one case with rootLayer or explicit payloadArcSites, optional payloadLayers map, and an ops array. The adapter may discover payload arc sites from rootLayer for tests, but the domain source consumes payload arc records rather than a concrete prior namespace-source provider.
adapter_protocol.output: A JSON object with ok, diagnostics, and results.
